column2: Apple's tablet operating system for iPad devices is {{c1::iPadOS}}.
column3: iPadOS is related to iOS but is presented separately in the Core 2 objective.
column4: Use this for platform recognition: iPad tablets map to iPadOS, while iPhones map to iOS.
column8: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.1; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, pp. 1-2
column9: A+::220-1202::1.1 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OperatingSystemTypesandPurposes Cloze MobileOS Apple Source::Messer-v140

column2: Apple's phone operating system for iPhones is {{c1::iOS}}.
column3: iOS is Apple-controlled and tied to Apple mobile devices.
column4: This is a compact platform association, not a detailed app-development card.
column8: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.1; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 2
column9: A+::220-1202::1.1 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OperatingSystemTypesandPurposes Cloze MobileOS Apple Source::Messer-v140

column2: Google Android is a mobile operating system based on {{c1::Linux}}.
column4: Do not treat Android as an Apple platform. The objective separates Android from iOS and iPadOS.
column8: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.1; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 2
column9: A+::220-1202::1.1 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OperatingSystemTypesandPurposes Cloze MobileOS Android Source::Messer-v140

column4: The acronym is useful, but the more important exam distinction is that NTFS is not the best general cross-platform removable-media choice.
column8: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.1; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 2
column9: A+::220-1202::1.1 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OperatingSystemTypesandPurposes Cloze Filesystems Windows Source::Messer-v140

column3: ReFS is associated with Windows storage resilience and large storage needs.
column4: Remember the "resilient" clue: ReFS is about integrity and large Windows storage, not broad removable-media compatibility.
column8: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.1; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 2
column9: A+::220-1202::1.1 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OperatingSystemTypesandPurposes Cloze Filesystems Windows Source::Messer-v140

column2: FAT32 has a maximum individual file size of about {{c1::4 GB}}.
column3: This limitation is why FAT32 is a poor fit for large-file removable storage.
column4: This is a common trap when a scenario mentions broad compatibility but also requires files larger than 4 GB.
column8: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.1; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 2
column9: A+::220-1202::1.1 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OperatingSystemTypesandPurposes Cloze HighYield Filesystems Source::Messer-v140

column3: exFAT is commonly used for removable media that must work across multiple operating systems.
column4: The acronym matters less than the use case: exFAT is the cross-platform flash drive answer when large files are involved.
column8: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.1; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 2
column9: A+::220-1202::1.1 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OperatingSystemTypesandPurposes Cloze Filesystems Compatibility Source::Messer-v140

column3: APFS is used with modern Apple operating systems, including macOS, iOS, and iPadOS.
column4: APFS is the Apple filesystem association. Do not choose it for a generic Windows/Linux/macOS flash-drive sharing scenario.
column8: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.1; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 2
column9: A+::220-1202::1.1 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OperatingSystemTypesandPurposes Cloze Filesystems Apple Source::Messer-v140
